tools.description: Create a new GitHub issue for tracking bugs, feature requests, or tasks. Use this for actionable work items that need assignment, labeling, and status tracking. For reports, announcements, or status updates that don't require task tracking, use create_discussion instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created.

body.description: Detailed issue description in Markdown. Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. Include context, reproduction steps, or acceptance criteria as appropriate.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

labels.description: Labels to categorize the issue (e.g., 'bug', 'enhancement'). Labels must exist in the repository.

parent.description: Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Use '#aw_ID' in body text to reference other issues by their temporary_id; these are replaced with actual issue numbers after creation.

title.description: Concise issue title summarizing the bug, feature, or task. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.

tools.name: create_issue

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) ŌĆö it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Dispatch the issue triage workflow for the newly created issue
tools.name: dispatch_triage

tools.description: Get details for a commit from a GitHub repository

include_diff.description: Whether to include file diffs and stats in the response. Default is true.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

sha.description: Commit SHA, branch name, or tag name

tools.name: get_commit

tools.description: Get the contents of a file or directory from a GitHub repository

ref.description: Accepts optional git refs such as `refs/tags/{tag}`, `refs/heads/{branch}` or `refs/pull/{pr_number}/head`

sha.description: Accepts optional commit SHA. If specified, it will be used instead of ref

tools.name: get_file_contents

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get the latest release in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_latest_release

annotations.title: Get a release by tag name

tools.description: Get a specific release by its tag name in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_release_by_tag

tools.description: Get details about a specific git tag in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_tag

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List branches in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_branches

tools.description: Get list of commits of a branch in a GitHub repository. Returns at least 30 results per page by default, but can return more if specified using the perPage parameter (up to 100).

author.description: Author username or email address to filter commits by

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

sha.description: Commit SHA, branch or tag name to list commits of. If not provided, uses the default branch of the repository. If a commit SHA is provided, will list commits up to that SHA.

tools.name: list_commits

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: List releases in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_releases

tools.description: List git tags in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_tags

tools.description: Fast and precise code search across ALL GitHub repositories using GitHub's native search engine. Best for finding exact symbols, functions, classes, or specific code patterns.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub's powerful code search syntax. Examples: 'content:Skill language:Java org:github', 'NOT is:archived language:Python OR language:go', 'repo:github/github-mcp-server'. Supports exact matching, language filters, path filters, and more.

tools.name: search_code

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

tools.description: Find GitHub repositories by name, description, readme, topics, or other metadata. Perfect for discovering projects, finding examples, or locating specific repositories across GitHub.

minimal_output.description: Return minimal repository information (default: true). When false, returns full GitHub API repository objects.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Repository search query. Examples: 'machine learning in:name stars:>1000 language:python', 'topic:react', 'user:facebook'. Supports advanced search syntax for precise filtering.

sort.description: Sort repositories by field, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_repositories

[{"sha":"[REDACTED]","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/commit/[REDACTED]","commit":{"message":"Migrate Azure.AI.Translation.Document to new generator (#56970)

* update tsp-location and project file

* regenerate

* regenerate tests

* update custom code

* Rename Model Factory

* Fix build errors and regenerate

* Update tests

* Export API

* Update library inventory

* regenerate

* Update Library inventory after merge

* Revert change in parameter name for GetTranslationStatus

* Make Translate and TranslateAsync signatures public

* Apply copilot's suggestions

* Export API

* Fix tests diagnostic scopes

* Fix filename casing for MultiPartFormDataRequestContent.cs

The file was tracked as MultipartFormDataRequestContent.cs (lowercase p) in git
but the generator produces MultiPartFormDataRequestContent.cs (uppercase P).
On Windows this is invisible, but on Linux CI it shows as a deletion + new file,
causing the 'Generated code is not up to date' CI failure.

* Update library inventory

* Remove custom code for StartTranslation

* Regenerate

* Update credential logic in custom layer after regenerating

* Add ExperimentalAttribute to project compilation sources

* Export API

* Update library inventory

* Update DocumentTranslationClientTests with null options

* Update library inventory after merge

* Remove ForwardsClientCalls attribute and regenerate

* Add diagnosticScope param in Pageable methods

* Fix the diagnostic scopes

* skipping file search tests for now","author":{"name":"M-Hietala","email":"78813398+M-Hietala@users.noreply.github.com","date":"2026-04-07T21:42:17Z"},"committer":{"name":"GitHub","email":"noreply@github.com","date":"2026-04-07T21:42:17Z"}},"author":{"login":"M-Hietala","id":78813398,"profile_url":"https://github.com/M-Hietala","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/78813398?v=4"},"committer":{"login":"web-flow","id":19864447,"profile_url":"https://github.com/web-flow","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19864447?v=4"}},{"sha":"[REDACTED]","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/commit/[REDACTED]","commit":{"message":"Move FoundryEnrichmentProcessor agent tags to OnEnd (#57877)

Move gen_ai.agent.name, gen_ai.agent.version, and gen_ai.agent.id from
OnStart to OnEnd so that underlying frameworks (e.g. OpenAI SDK) cannot
overwrite them mid-span. microsoft.foundry.project.id remains in OnStart.

Tests rewritten to use a real TracerProvider + ActivitySource pipeline
and include overwrite-resistance assertions.

resource.text:
# Release History

## 2.1.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)

### Features Added

### Breaking Changes

### Bugs Fixed

### Other Changes

## 2.0.0 (2024-11-15)

### Features Added

- Exposed `JsonModelWriteCore` for model serialization procedure.


### Other Changes
- Renamed SingleDocumentTranslationClient's API from `document_translate` to `translate`

## 2.0.0-beta.2 (2024-07-05)

### Features Added
- Single document translation client can be created using endpoint alone, mainly for SDK to work against containers.

### Bugs Fixed
- SourceInput options which is a part of TranslationInput is now public. This allows users to enter source language, source storage, and document filter prefix and suffix.

## 2.0.0-beta.1 (2024-05-07)

### Features Added
- `documenttranslate` is the method added to submit a single/synchronous document translation request to the Document Translation service.
- `DocumentTranslateContent` has been added to specify the `documenttranslate` request content.
- Added `getSupportedFormats` that returns a list of supported documents and glossaries by the Document Translation service.

### Breaking Changes
- Version `v1.0` is not supported
- Renamed property `FormatType` to `FileFormatType`

## 1.0.0 (2022-06-07)

### Features Added
- Added optional parameter `categoryId` to the `DocumentTranslationInput.AddTarget`.
- Added property `Ascending` to type `DocumentFilterOrder` and `TranslationFilterOrder`.
- `DocumentTranslationAudience` has been added to allow the user to select the Azure cloud where the resource is located.

### Breaking Changes
- Renamed type `StorageInputType` to `StorageInputUriKind`.
- Renamed property `StorageType` to `StorageUriKind` in `DocumentTranslationInput`.
- Renamed parameter `asc` to `ascending` in `TranslationFilterOrder` constructor.
- The following properties inside of the `DocumentTranslationOperation` class will throw an `InvalidOperationException` if they are accessed and the LRO hasn't made a request to the service:
  - `CreatedOn`
  - `DocumentsCanceled`
  - `DocumentsFailed`
  - `DocumentsInProgress`
  - `DocumentsNotStarted`
  - `DocumentsSucceeded`
  - `DocumentsTotal`
  - `LastModified`
  - `Status`

### Bugs Fixed
- In `DocumentTranslationOperation`, `Cancel` calls won't overwrite the response from `GetRawResponse` anymore.

## 1.0.0-beta.6 (2021-11-09)

### Breaking Changes
- Removed types `DocumentTranslationError` and `DocumentTranslationErrorCode`. These affected the classes `DocumentStatusResult` and `TranslationStatusResult`. Errors in both classes are now exposed as `ResponseError`.
- Renamed method `DocumentStatus` to `DocumentStatusResult` in `DocumentTranslationModelFactory`, which now takes a `BinaryData` type instead of `DocumentTranslationError`.
- Renamed method `TranslationStatus` to `TranslationStatusResult` in `DocumentTranslationModelFactory`, which now takes a `BinaryData` type instead of `DocumentTranslationError`.

## 1.0.0-beta.5 (2021-09-08)

### Breaking Changes
- `DocumentFilter.CreatedAfter` and `DocumentFilter.CreatedBefore` are now nullable properties.
- Renamed method `GetAllTranslationStatuses` to `GetTranslationStatuses`.
- Renamed method `GetAllDocumentStatuses` to `GetDocumentStatuses`.
- Renamed type `TranslationFilter` to `GetTranslationStatusesOptions`.
- Renamed type `DocumentFilter` to `GetDocumentStatusesOptions`.
- Renamed type `DocumentStatus` to `DocumentStatusResult`.
- Renamed type `TranslationStatus` to `TranslationStatusResult`.
- Renamed type `FileFormat` to `DocumentTranslationFileFormat`.
- Renamed property `TranslatedTo` to `TranslatedToLanguageCode` in `DocumentStatusResult`.
- Renamed parameter `asc` to `ascending` in `DocumentStatusResult` constructor.
- Changed spelling of `cancelled`/`cancelling` to `canceled`/`cancelling`. The following changes have been made:
  - property `DocumentsCancelled` to `DocumentsCanceled` in `DocumentTranslationOperation`
  - property `DocumentsCancelled` to `DocumentsCanceled` in `TranslationStatusResult`
  - value `Cancelled` to `Canceled` in `DocumentTranslationOperation`
  - value `Cancelling` to `Canceling` in `DocumentTranslationOperation`
  - parameter `Cancelled` to `Canceled` in function `DocumentTranslationModelFactory.TranslationStatus` 

### Bugs Fixed
- `GetDocumentStasus` and `GetDocumentStatusesAsync` no longer set CreatedAfter and CreatedBefore if the user doesn't set it.
- In `GetTranslationStatuses`, `GetDocumentStatuses` and their async counterparts; URL parameters for `Ids`, `Statuses`, and `OrderBy` are no longer included in the request URL if the user does not set them.

## 1.0.0-beta.4 (2021-08-10)

### Features Added
- Added filtering options to methods `GetAllTranslationStatuses`, `GetAllDocumentStatuses` and their async equivalent.
- Added `DocumentTranslationModelFactory` static class to support mocking model types. 

### Breaking Changes
- Properties `Suffix` and `Prefix` are now available under `TranslationSource` instead of in type `DocumentFilter`.
- Type `DocumentTranslationError` is now a struct.
- Type `StorageInputType` is now a regular enum instead of an extensible enum.

## 1.0.0-beta.3 (2021-07-07)
### Breaking changes

- Renamed property `DocumentId` to `Id`in `DocumentStatusResult`.
- Renamed property `TranslationId` to `Id`in `TranslationStatusResult`
- Renamed type `TranslationStatusResult` to `TranslationStatus`.
- Renamed type `DocumentStatusResult` to `DocumentStatus`.
- Renamed enum `TranslationStatus` to `DocumentTranslationStatus`.
- Renamed method `GetDocumentFormats` to `GetSupportedDocumentFormats`.
- Renamed method `GetGlossaryFormats` to `GetSupportedGlossaryFormats`.
- Removed property `HasCompleted` from types `DocumentStatusResult` and `TranslationStatusResult`.

## 1.0.0-beta.2 (2021-06-08)

### New Features

- Added support for authentication with Azure Active Directory.

### Breaking changes

- This version of the SDK defaults to the latest supported service version, which currently is `v1.0`.
- Renamed method `GetTranslations` to `GetAllTranslationStatuses` and same for the async equivalent method.
- Renamed property `TranslateTo` to `TranslatedTo` in type `DocumentStatusResult`.

## 1.0.0-beta.1 (2021-04-06)

This is the first beta package of the Azure.AI.Translation.Document client library that targets the service version `1.0-preview.1`.
This package's documentation and samples demonstrate the new API.

arguments.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.Translation.Document` **Service directory:** `sdk/translation/Azure.AI.Translation.Document/` **Triggered by:** PR #56970 (commit 085c6ca) by @JonathanCrd ## What Changed PR #56970 migrated `Azure.AI.Translation.Document` to the new Azure Data Plane SDK generator. The changes include: - Migrated codegen to use the new TypeSpec-based generator (updated `tsp-location.yaml` and project file) - Renamed `DocumentTranslationModelFactory` internal structure (Model Factory rename) - Custom code for `StartTranslation` removed ŌĆö now fully generated - Credential logic in the custom layer updated after regeneration - `Translate` and `TranslateAsync` methods made public in the custom layer - `ForwardsClientCalls` attribute removed from client methods - `ExperimentalAttribute` added to project compilation sources - Diagnostic scopes fixed in Pageable methods ## Gaps Found - **CHANGELOG `2.1.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` is completely empty** ŌĆö all subsections ("Features Added", "Breaking Changes", "Bugs Fixed", "Other Changes") are blank despite significant changes landing in this version - **README does not mention `ExperimentalAttribute`** ŌĆö the library now marks itself as experimental but there is no note for users in the README about this status or what it implies - **README "Authenticate the client" section still only shows `AzureKeyCredential` and `DefaultAzureCredential`** ŌĆö the updated credential logic in the custom layer (post-regeneration) is not reflected with a note about any behavioral changes - **No mention of `SingleDocumentTranslationClient.Translate` / `TranslateAsync` visibility change** ŌĆö these methods are now public (custom layer) but this change is not surfaced in docs or changelog
­¤ōÉ Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### File 1: `sdk/translation/Azure.AI.Translation.Document/CHANGELOG.md` Edit the `## 2.1.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` section. Replace the empty body with entries reflecting the actual changes from PR #56970: ```markdown ## 2.1.0-beta.1 (Unreleased) ### Features Added - `SingleDocumentTranslationClient.Translate` and `SingleDocumentTranslationClient.TranslateAsync` are now publicly accessible. ### Breaking Changes - The library is now generated using the new Azure TypeSpec-based SDK generator. Some internal implementation details have changed, though the public API surface remains largely compatible with 2.0.0. - `ForwardsClientCalls` attribute has been removed from client methods. ### Other Changes - Migrated code generation to the new TypeSpec-based Azure SDK generator for improved maintainability. - Updated credential handling logic in the client custom layer. - `ExperimentalAttribute` has been added to the package; this library is under active development and the API surface may change. ``` #### File 2: `sdk/translation/Azure.AI.Translation.Document/README.md` Add a note at the top of the **Getting started** section (right after the introductory paragraph and the badge links, before the `## Getting started` heading) to inform users of the experimental status: ```markdown > **Note:** This library is currently in an experimental state. The API surface may change in future releases. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new snippet test files need to be created for these changes ŌĆö the existing samples in `sdk/translation/Azure.AI.Translation.Document/tests/Samples/` cover the main usage patterns. However, if the agent wants to add a snippet showing `SingleDocumentTranslationClient.Translate` usage, follow this structure: 1. **Write sample code** in `sdk/translation/Azure.AI.Translation.Document/tests/Samples/Sample6_SingleDocumentTranslation.cs` (check if it already exists; if so, update it): - Wrap the `Translate` method example with `#region Snippet:SingleDocumentTranslation` / `#endregion` - Add `[Test]` attribute 2. **Add placeholder in README** in the `## Examples` section under a new heading `### Translate a single document`: ``` ```C# Snippet:SingleDocumentTranslation ``` ``` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README currently contains all required sections. Verify each is complete: 1. Ō£ģ Getting started (install, prerequisites, authenticate) 2. Ō£ģ Key concepts 3. Ō£ģ Examples (async and sync) 4. Ō£ģ Troubleshooting (via reference links) 5. Ō£ģ Next steps 6. Ō£ģ Contributing The main gap is the missing experimental status note and the incomplete CHANGELOG. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/translation/Azure.AI.Translation.Document/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/translation/Azure.AI.Translation.Document/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 translation` 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 translation` 5. `dotnet format sdk/translation/Azure.AI.Translation.Document/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
arguments.title: [Translation] Docs: Update CHANGELOG and README after generator migration
